Archive / Issue #47

Spring Sounds & Cherry Blossoms

Wednesday, March 18, 2026

Editor's Note

"Spring came in fast this year. One day it was -12 and grey, the next the cherry trees along the Canal were doing their thing. This is the Ottawa I love — no preamble, just suddenly beautiful. Enjoy this one."
Featured This Week

Ottawa Jazz Festival – Spring Preview Night

Saturday, March 21 · 7:30 PMNAC Southam Hall, 1 Elgin St

The city's favourite jazz celebration kicks off its spring edition with an intimate preview night. Local legend Colleen Allen leads a quartet through standards and originals, joined by a Montreal bassist making his Ottawa debut. Drinks in the lobby from 6:30.

Get Tickets & Details

Getting this from a friend?

Subscribe free and get Ottawa's best in your inbox every Thursday.

Free, every Thursday. Unsubscribe anytime.

This Week's Highlights

Dow's Lake Cherry Blossom Walk

All weekend · Dawn to duskDow's Lake Pavilion, 1001 Queen Elizabeth Dr

Peak bloom is here. The ornamental cherry trees along the Canal are absolutely putting on a show this year — experts are calling it the best season in a decade. Go early to beat the crowds. Free, always.

More info

Spring Craft Beer Festival

Friday–Sunday · Various timesLansdowne Park, Aberdeen Pavilion

Over 40 Ontario craft breweries pour their spring seasonals. Try the wild ales, fruited sours, and the smoked maple porter from Beau's that's getting serious attention this year.

More info

Carp Farmers' Market – Opening Day

Saturday, March 21 · 8:00 AM – 1:00 PMCarp Fairgrounds, 3790 Carp Rd

One of the Ottawa Valley's most beloved markets reopens for the season. Fresh maple syrup, greenhouse seedlings, artisan bread, and 80+ vendors. Get there before 10 or the good stuff disappears.

More info

Gatineau Park Wildflower Walk

Sunday, March 22 · 10:00 AMPink Lake Trailhead, Gatineau Park

A guided 90-minute walk through the park's best early wildflower spots — trout lilies, bloodroot, hepatica. Led by NCC naturalists. Free with registration, limited to 20 participants.

More info

Family Corner

Dino Discovery Days at the Museum of Nature

Saturday & Sunday · 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM·Canadian Museum of Nature, 240 McLeod St

Hands-on fossil activities, dino puppet shows, and a chance to meet real paleontologists working on the new Cretaceous gallery. Ages 4–12, included with museum admission.

More info

Family Paint Night – Spring Edition

Saturday, March 21 · 2:00 PM·Hintonburg Community Centre, 1064 Wellington St W

Drop-in family painting workshop. Kids paint cherry blossoms while parents relax with a coffee. All supplies included, just $5 per family. No registration needed.

More info

Saturday Roller Skating at Rideau

Saturdays · 2:00 PM – 5:00 PM·Rideau Arena, 1505 Rideau St

Classic Saturday afternoon roller skating with a DJ spinning crowd-pleasers. Skate rentals available on-site. Ages 5 and up, $6 entry.

More info
